result:
The build is clean. The implementation is complete:

- **`EscapeField`** — returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it doubles internal quotes and wraps the result in double-quotes.
- **`JoinRow`** — escapes each field and joins with commas; an empty list yields the empty string via `string.Join`.
- **`SplitRow`** — a single-pass parser: a field starting with `"` is consumed until the un-doubled closing quote (doubled quotes contribute one literal `"`, commas/CR/LF inside are content); otherwise the field runs to the next comma or end of line. The loop structure naturally handles the edge cases the spec pins down — an empty line parses to `[""]`, and a trailing comma appends a final empty field — so `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields))` round-trips every non-empty list.

All three operations are pure static methods on `CsvRow` alongside the untouched `NeedsQuoting`, and `dotnet build` succeeds with no warnings or errors.
